title: [release-1.3] Cirrus: Disable OSX task
body:
Release-branches infrequently change, but the OSX VM images constantly
change.  Disable this test to avoid encountering flakes.

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body: @mtrmac @vrothberg do we need to update 1.3 branch at all ?

> @mtrmac @vrothberg do we need to update 1.3 branch at all ?

That’s a good question. There’s probably a mapping of maintained RPM releases vs. upstream release branches somewhere, but I don’t know where that is.

> There’s probably a mapping of maintained RPM releases

So long as those RPMs are outside of any support commitments, they will never need to be built again.  Since the solution to any problems is: upgrade to a newer version.  Unf. I'm also unaware of where/how/who uses this branch downstream :confused: 

Maybe @TomSweeneyRedHat or @lsm5 knows?  

Ref: In podman-CI-land, we essentially have three branch/CI states:
* Fully active and supported downstream packages and/or lots of code churn:  Fully active CI Testing
* Still supported packages, but almost zero / very low code churn: Partially active CI, check the code compiles and skip all actual testing.
* Fully dead/unsupported and zero code changes: Fully disabled/inactivated CI.  Nothing runs.

Historically, with Skopeo everything has happened on the `main` branch, with release branches only made for security backports. Skopeo is simple enough that we didn’t, so far, need to branch at a pre-release to stabilize; so “fully active branches with lots of churn” has only ever been `main`.
